message:
fuzz: add fuzz_traversal_dominance target with rich invariants

New structure-aware fuzz target exercising:

- fnx_algorithms::bfs_edges, bfs_layers, descendants_at_distance
- fnx_algorithms::dfs_edges
- fnx_algorithms::immediate_dominators, dominance_frontiers
- fnx_algorithms::moral_graph

Beyond no-panic, asserts these runtime contracts:

BFS family
  - Tree-shape: bfs_edges yields exactly one edge per non-source
    target (target endpoints are pairwise distinct).
  - Layer partition: every node listed by bfs_layers appears in
    exactly one layer; the source sits in layer 0.
  - distance-0 invariant: descendants_at_distance(_, s, 0) == {s}.
  - Layer ↔ distance equivalence: a node is in layer k iff
    descendants_at_distance(_, s, k) contains it.

DFS family
  - Tree-shape: dfs_edges yields a forest whose target endpoints
    are pairwise distinct.

Dominance family (digraph)
  - Function: every key of immediate_dominators(_, s) maps to a
    unique parent in the graph.
  - Acyclic: parent-pointer chains terminate within ≤ |idom| + 2
    steps, ruling out cycles.
  - Containment: idom keys and parents are in the graph;
    dominance_frontiers keys and frontier elements are in the graph.

Moral family
  - moral_graph supersets the underlying skeleton (every directed
    edge survives in some orientation).

Includes a coverage-minimized seed corpus of 340 inputs harvested
from a 60s libfuzzer pass + merge=1 minimization (2116 cov edges,
7773 features).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+doc = false
